results.title: Code the Past Hackathon : Innovate. Collaborate. Shape The Past. - Devpost
excerpts:
* **Protect the planet** – sustainability, clean tech, or conservation solutions.
* **Push boundaries** – explore space, robotics, AI, or emerging technology.

No matter what you build, your goal is to make it **impactful, innovative, and inspiring** — just like the work we do in the _FIRST Tech Challenge_ .

#### What to Submit

**What to Submit**

Your final submission will be a **3–5 minute video** showcasing your project. In your video, be sure to:

1. **Introduce Your Team** – Names, roles, and where you’re from.
2. **Present the Problem** – What challenge are you addressing, and why does it matter?
3. **Show Your Solution** – Demonstrate your prototype, code, or design in action.
4. **Explain the Tech** – Briefly describe how it works and the tools you used.
5. **Highlight the Impact** – How could this solution help people, communities, or the world?

**Format:** MP4 or YouTube link (unlisted or public).  
**Deadline:** Will be announced after the challenge kickoff.
Your submission doesn’t need to be heavily edited — clarity and creativity matter more than flashy production. Tell your story, show your work, and inspire others.

### Hackathon Sponsors

### Prizes

$ 200 in prizes

###### 1st place

$ 100 in cash

1 winner

$ 75 in cash

1 winner

1 winner

results.title: WeCracked NextJS Hackathon: Learn more about NextJS by creating a project with niche NextJS features - Devpost
excerpts:
There's always more to discover, so keep exploring!

**About WeCracked:**

WeCracked is committed to helping students learn and prepare for hackathons. We offer a unique approach to prizes through travel reimbursements for promising participants.

**WeCracked Hackathons:**

Our hackathons are designed to prepare you for future hackathons by providing valuable feedback from judges. Teams that excel in our hackathons will recieve future travel reimbursements credits.

**Travel Reimbursement System:**

Winners will receive travel credits that can be redeemed in your online portal on the [WeCracked](https://wecracked.com/) website. WeCracked will update your available credits in our internal system for you after you win one of our hackathons. To redeem your credits, simply upload a receipt of your travel expenses, and after review, the reimbursement will be sent to you via Venmo or Zelle.

**Why Travel Reimbursements:**
Rather than cash prizes, we offer travel reimbursements to dedicated students to lessen the financial burden of travel to non-local hackathons. This ensures funds directly support our goal and prevents misuse. Only active community contributors qualify, maintaining integrity and genuine involvement.

### Requirements

\- Project and video submission deadline is July 7th, 12:00 PM EST

\- Your teams GitHub repo must be present in your submission

\- Previous projects are not allowed

### Hackathon Sponsors

### Prizes

$ 120 in prizes

###### $20 Travel Reimbursement

1 winner

$20 in travel reimbursement credit per team member for future hackathons.

###### $10 Travel Reimbursement

1 winner

$10 in travel reimbursement credit per team member for future hackathons.

###### $5 Travel Reimbursement

1 winner

$5 travel reimbursement per team member for future hackathons.

### Devpost Achievements

Submitting to this hackathon could earn you:

### Judges

**Bill Zhang**
_Co-Founder @ WeCracked_

**Kevin Wu**  
_Intern @ StokeSpace_

**Simon Quach**  
_Intern @ CAIDA_

**Chinat Yu**  
_MLH Top 50_

**Kieran Llarena**

**William Wang**  
_Founder @ HackCanada_

### Judging Criteria

* **NextJS Features**  
  The number of NextJS features utilized
* **Creativity**  
  The creativity and effectiveness of feature implementation
* **Quality**  
  Code quality and best practices
* **Coherence**  
  Overall project coherence. Features should not be added for the sake of being added.

Questions? Email the hackathon manager

results.title: Quick Emergency Responses with AI: Developer Spotlight
excerpts:
During critical emergency situations such as natural calamities, the emergency services like 911 receive hundreds and thousands of requests for urgent assistance. A shortage of call centers' staff at such times may hinder the ability to mitigate critical emergencies. An [Intel® Student Ambassador](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/training/academic-program/student-ambassador.html) developed an empathic [Generative AI (GenAI)](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/topic-technology/artificial-intelligence/training/generative-ai.html) solution called [Dispatch AI](https://devpost.com/software/dispatch-ai) leveraging Intel’s latest accelerated hardware, [AI tools](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/ai-analytics-toolkit.html) and [frameworks](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/frameworks/overview.html) on [Intel® Tiber™ Developer Cloud](https://www.intel.
com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/devcloud/services.html) platform to revolutionize emergency responses, providing emotion-aware assistance to both the calls and the dispatchers of the emergency call services.

The Dispatch AI project won multiple prizes at the [University of California, Berkeley’s AI Hackathon](https://community.intel.com/t5/Blogs/Tech-Innovation/Artificial-Intelligence-AI/Intel-sponsored-UC-Berkeley-s-AI-Hackathon/post/1613966) held on June 22-23, 2024.

### **About the Intel® Student Ambassador**

[Bill Zhang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bill-zhang1/) is currently pursuing a Masters in Computer Science degree with a focus on AI at the University of Southern California. His journey as an Intel Student Ambassador showcases the power of combining innovative ideas with Intel's advanced cloud computing and AI technologies to create solutions that can potentially save lives and revolutionize industries.

### **About the Dispatch AI Project**
In just 27 hours during the UC Berkeley's AI hackathon, Bill and his team integrated AI agents, LLM evaluations, and physical phone calling into a comprehensive SaaS platform. Dispatch AI processes emergency calls through Twilio\*, utilizing a custom Mistral AI LLM fine-tuned on Intel Tiber Developer Cloud to generate responses. Simultaneously, the system employs OpenAI\* GPT-4 to extract critical information from call transcripts. The team also incorporated emotion detection feature into the solution for more empathetic call responses.

The interactive UI of Dispatch AI looks as follows:

**Fig.1:** User interface of Dispatch AI

### **Architecture of Dispatch AI**

Following is a comprehensive architecture design of Dispatch AI that helps visualize the communication flow across various components of the hardware and software stack used in the project:

**Fig.2:** Communication flow across Dispatch AI’s architecture

### **Intel’s AI Tools and Technologies Used**

# Bill Zhang - AI Engineer & Developer Portfolio

Bill Zhang is pursuing a Master of Science in Computer Science with a focus on Artificial Intelligence at USC, following a Bachelor's degree from UC Santa Cruz.
He has experience as an AI Engineer at RingCentral, where he automated support chat analysis and developed a web chatbot, and has completed an internship as a Software Engineer at SproutLabs. His projects include award-winning applications in real-time bus tracking, emergency call triaging, and ASL recognition, showcasing his technical skills in various programming languages and machine learning frameworks.

results.title: Building MirAI (HackIllinois 2025) - Bill Zhang
excerpts:
[Bill Zhang](https://www.billjzhang.com/)

Home All posts About Me Tags

# Building MirAI (HackIllinois 2025)

Posted on Mar 23, 2025

I mentioned before on this website that I wanted to keep a record of the projects I’ve done. Not only can I eventually look back and see what progess I’ve made as a developer (“wow, I used to write _really_ bad code”), but I also want to be able to read this and get up to speed with the project in case I ever want to work on it again. I don’t really keep documentation of my projects (bad practice, I know), so this will come in handy for me.

Today, I’ll be explaining how me and my team build MirAI during HackIllinois 2025, a 36 hour hackathon at UIUC.

## What is MirAI?

MirAI is an AI-powered jira/task board (read: gpt wrapper) that can take an idea, generate tickets for it, host virtual stand up, and then modify the task board and track progress. The name MirAI comes from the japanese word Mirai 未来, meaning future.
We could’ve also went with the same word but in Chinese, which is WeiLai 未来 (spelled the same in both languages), but I already knew a company called [WeiLai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nio_Inc.) so I didn’t want to use the same word.

We chose this project because we wanted to submit a project under the “Dev Tool” track (we thought we had the best chance of winning under this track).

## Features (MVP)

We had a set of features we _needed_ to complete before time was up (the minimum viable product MVP), and the features are as follows:

* The MVP should take a project idea, deadlines, titles, descriptions, a set of people on your team and their qualifications (title, yeas of experience, etc) and generate a list of tickets that you can follow to complete your project
* Each ticket needs to have an ID, and can be freely moved to different categories of progress.
The categories we had for progress include To Do, In Progress, QA (quality assurance), PR (pull request), and Closed.
* There should be a way to simulate standup (like in most agile workflows), and after each person gives updates to MirAI, it should be able to rearrange or edit the tickets so that progess flows smoothly. We wanted to this be as dynamic as possible, so that means if someone was sick, either their ticket gets assigned to someone else or the deadline gets extended. Or another case would be if someone on the team gets laid off, or if people join, the board should be able to reassign some of the heavier tickets (if needed) or add people to existing ones.
* Tickets should be linked to the project’s Github repo. This is copying the feature that Jira already has (but for Github). Essentially, if you are working on ticket #1280, you should include “1280” in your commits, and MirAI will track those and will even be able to give feedback on your code quality.

Bill Zhang is a Business Development And Management In Pursuit For Entrepreneurial Venture at TCL based in Huizhou, Guangdong. Explore more ## Bill Zhang Workplace

TCL

### Company Background

TCL is a global electronics manufacturer founded in 1999 that specializes in producing consumer electronics and appliances. The company is vertically integrated, manufacturing its own components and controlling the entire production process from design to distribution. This business model enables TCL to offer high-quality electronic products with advanced technology at competitive prices. TCL operates across multiple continents including Asia, North America, Europe, Australia, and the Middle East, serving both business and consumer markets with a diverse product portfolio.
